common_subject: Free compound interest calculator/widget for your readers
common_offer: Free embeddable compound interest calculator via iframe
common_cta: Implicit — embed code included, no explicit ask
template_used: widget-outreach.cjs template — generic 2-sentence intro + embed code + 'No obligation' close
overall_verdict: MASS FAILURE — 0/20 replies. Wrong targets, weak offer match, generic subject, no personalization.

entries.authority_relevance: HIGH site authority, but they BUILD their own calculators — no need for external widget
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.personalization_level: NONE — generic template, wrong contact (creditcards@ not editorial)
entries.likely_failure_reason: NerdWallet builds proprietary calculators and has no incentive to embed external widgets. Wrong contact (credit cards team, not editorial).

entries.authority_relevance: Medium — relevant audience, but generic contact
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Generic subject with no article/page reference. Compound interest is too broad — no specific angle for their audience.

entries.authority_relevance: HIGH authority but STRONG brand calculator ecosystem (EveryDollar, mortgage payoff, investment)
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Dave Ramsey has a proprietary calculator ecosystem and branded tools. Would never embed a third-party calculator. Wrong target entirely.

entries.authority_relevance: Medium — relevant audience, smaller site more likely to engage
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Generic outreach with no reference to any specific article. Offer mismatched — blog is about saving/budgeting, not compound interest.

entries.authority_relevance: Medium — financial literacy focus
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: No specific article reference. Generic template doesn't show you've read their content.

entries.site_type: Calculator DIRECTOR (DR ~75). They ARE the calculator.
entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — they are a competitor, not a publisher
entries.subject: Free embeddable compound interest calculator
entries.likely_failure_reason: Calculator.net IS a calculator website. They make their own. Pitching them to embed someone else's calculator is nonsensical.

entries.authority_relevance: HIGH but they have their own calculator suite
entries.subject: Free Compound Interest Widget for Your Readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Bankrate has an extensive proprietary calculator suite. Would never embed third-party. Wrong target.

entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — they BUILD calculators for businesses
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your blog
entries.likely_failure_reason: CalcXML sells embeddable calculators to enterprises. Pitching them to embed ours is like asking Coca-Cola to serve Pepsi.

entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — direct competitor with own calculator suite
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: SmartAsset has its own calculator business. Direct competitor. Completely wrong target.

entries.site_type: Calculator website (DR ~65). Their ENTIRE product IS calculators.
entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — competitor
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Another calculator website. They ARE the calculator, not a publisher looking for tools.

entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — large fintech with own tools
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Credit Karma has its own integrated calculator suite. Massive company. Wrong target and wrong contact (feedback@, not content/editorial).

entries.site_type: Calculator platform (DR ~75). They ARE the calculator industry.
entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — direct competitor
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your blog
entries.likely_failure_reason: Omni Calculator is the world's largest embeddable calculator platform. Asking them to embed ours is absurd.

entries.authority_relevance: Medium-High — mortgage-focused publisher, BUT they have their own calculators
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.offer: Embeddable compound interest widget (NOT mortgage, despite being mortgage site)
entries.likely_failure_reason: Mortgage site was offered a compound interest widget, not a mortgage calculator. Complete offer mismatch. Should have pitched mortgage affordability.

entries.authority_relevance: High — perfect audience for mortgage calculator, BUT wrong offer
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Mortgage site offered a compound interest widget. Should have offered mortgage affordability widget. Offer mismatch killed relevance.

entries.authority_relevance: Medium — mortgage focused, but has own calculators
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Mortgage site, compound interest offer. Plus they have their own calculators. Wrong offer.

entries.authority_relevance: LOW — has own calculator section
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Money.com has its own calculators section. Large media brand unlikely to embed third-party widgets.

entries.authority_relevance: ZERO — Rocket Mortgage ecosystem. Their calculators drive leads.
entries.subject: Free compound interest widget for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Rocket Homes calculators are lead-generation funnels. Embedding third-party would cannibalize their business model.

entries.authority_relevance: LOW — Intuit ecosystem
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Intuit ecosystem. Corporate blog. Generic outreach to a trillion-dollar company. Never getting a reply.

entries.authority_relevance: LOW — SaaS company, not a content publisher
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: YNAB is budgeting software. Compound interest calculator is not relevant to their core value prop. Support@ contact, not editorial.

entries.authority_relevance: Medium-High — this was one of the better targets
entries.subject: Free compound interest calculator for your readers
entries.likely_failure_reason: Decent target but zero personalization. No article reference. No specific value prop. Lost in inbox noise.

key_insights: 12/20 targets were WRONG — competitors, calculator platforms, or massive brands that would never embed
20/20 emails had ZERO personalization — all used the same template from widget-outreach.cjs
20/20 subjects were generic variations of 'Free [widget] for your readers'
13/20 offers were mismatched to the site's actual content focus
9/20 targets were too large (DR 75+ major media/fintech) — these should have been excluded
Only ~3-4 targets were actually appropriate (Money Under 30, Making Sense of Cents, Personal Finance Club, maybe Mortgage Reports)
The automated widget-outreach.cjs engine treated ALL domains equally — no competitor filtering
Compound interest was the wrong primary offer — too generic for mortgage/debt sites
